ddls:
CREATE TABLE "websites" (
  "website_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each website */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the website */,
  "category_code" TEXT /* Code representing the category of the website */,
  "date_added" TEXT /* Date when the website was added to the system */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the data about the website */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Notes or comments about the website */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the website */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language of the website */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date when the website's information was last updated */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the website (e.g., active, archived, pending review) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("website_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_websites_source FOREIGN KEY ("source") REFERENCES sources ("source_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_websites_category_code FOREIGN KEY ("category_code") REFERENCES categories ("category_code")
)

CREATE TABLE "categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_code" TEXT /* Code representing the category */,
  "category_description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "parent_category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent category */,
  "category_keywords" TEXT /* Keywords associated with the category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the source */,
  "source_description" TEXT /* Description of the source */,
  "source_url" TEXT /* URL of the source */,
  "source_contact" TEXT /* Contact information for the source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date when the user last logged in */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "website_id" INTEGER /* ID of the website being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the website */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the website was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used for access */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_website_id FOREIGN KEY ("website_id") REFERENCES websites ("website_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "website_categories" (
  "website_id" INTEGER /* ID of the website */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("website_id", "category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "website_notes" (
  "note_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each note */,
  "website_id" INTEGER /* ID of the website */,
  "note_text" TEXT /* Text of the note */,
  "note_date" TEXT /* Date when the note was added */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who added the note */,
  "is_flagged" INTEGER /* Indicates if the note is flagged for review */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("note_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_website_notes_website_id FOREIGN KEY ("website_id") REFERENCES websites ("website_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_website_notes_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
